visa_types.name: Long Term Visa (D Type)
visa_types.documents_required:
- Fully complete Application Form
- 1 Photograph as per Standards
- Valid Passport and Copy of the Data page of the passport
- Confirmation of admission from the Austrian university/Institute, for Student/Pupil category.
- Visa Fees in Cash
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:  Long Term Visa (D Type) ]

[Table:  Long Term Visa (D Type) ]

Please Note: 
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of 31.76 per application in addition to the visa fees and must be paid in cash.
- All fees are non-refundable.
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.
Please note: The fees are to be paid in Euro and the notes must be undamaged.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The applicant will have to provide an approval email from Embassy as an evidence that he/she falls under this category. Kindly refer to the documents required page to make sure that you have all the supporting documents.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The applicant will have to provide an approval email from Embassy as an evidence that he/she falls under this category. Kindly refer to the documents required page to make sure that you have all the supporting documents.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Long Term Visa (D Type) ]

[Table:  Long Term Visa (D Type) ]

Please Note: 
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of 31.76 per application in addition to the visa fees and must be paid in cash.
- All fees are non-refundable.
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.
Please note: The fees are to be paid in Euro and the notes must be undamaged.

## Documents Required
- Fully complete Application Form
- 1 Photograph as per Standards
- Valid Passport and Copy of the Data page of the passport
- Confirmation of admission from the Austrian university/Institute, for Student/Pupil category.
- Visa Fees in Cash

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit their Application form with the new  photo format, as explained below: 
 The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO  (International Civil Aviation Organisation).
- Use       a plain light-coloured or white background.
- The       photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Portrait       style photo (no posing) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close       up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%       of the photograph.
- Photo       in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size 
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure: 
- The       picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A       smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels       by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On       the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3.5 cm x 4.5 cm       (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be       valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally,       the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.       Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120       Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An       image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used       to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better       printing quality.
- Photos       resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this       parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution,       the bigger the picture.
- The       only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format).       The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be       rejected.
Attaching the Photo to the Application form 
- Glue       one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their  parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father,  one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the  father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
 Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not  meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
Visa Application form

visa_types.name: Visiting Family and Friends
visa_types.documents_required:
- Download Checklist
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/irn/en/aut/travel-insurance)
- Documents showing that the applicant’s Iran residency with at least 3months validity more than the validity of the requested visa for applicant does not have Iranian citizenship.
- Visa application processing fee must be paid in euros. It should be noted in the case that the application is rejected, the paid fee will not be refunded
- Please click here to know more about the Visa Application Guideline

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. Kindly refer to the documents required page to make sure that you have all the supporting documents.
You qualify for this category if you have an invitation from a private person in Austria. That invitation can either be an electronic invitation (EVE) or a traditional letter of invitation
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. Kindly refer to the documents required page to make sure that you have all the supporting documents.
You qualify for this category if you have an invitation from a private person in Austria. That invitation can either be an electronic invitation (EVE) or a traditional letter of invitation

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Visiting Family and Friends]

[Table:  Visiting Family and Friends]

Please Note: 
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of 31.76 per application in addition to the visa fees and must be paid in cash.

- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.
Please note: The fees are to be paid in Euro and the notes must be undamaged.

- Download Checklist
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/irn/en/aut/travel-insurance)

- Visa application processing fee must be paid in euros. It should be noted in the case that the application is rejected, the paid fee will not be refunded
- Please click here to know more about the Visa Application Guideline

- Finally,       the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.       Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120       Kbytes.

- Photos       resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this       parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution,       the bigger the picture.
- The       only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format).       The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be       rejected.

- Glue       one photograph to the application form in the designated space.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.

visa_types.name: Individual Traveler
visa_types.documents_required:
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/irn/en/aut/travel-insurance)
Download Checklist
- Documents provided by the Visa Applicants must be in German or in English
- The application form which must be fully and legibly completed in German (in Latin letters) or in English by the applicant
- 01 color photos (not older than 6 months) (4.5 x  3.5 cm) click here for photo specification guide (http://www.passbildkriterien.at/oesterreich_neu.html)
- Valid passport (with at least 3months validity more than the validity of the requested visa, with at least 2 free pages, and not more than 10 years since its date of issuance)
- Original of previous passport
- Copy of the applicant's passport (the information page) and Copy of Previous Schengen visas
- Original work/job certificate in English or its translation in German or in English
- Copy Travel insurance policy (covering at least 30,000 euros, SARS,COVID 2 and repayment for the entire duration of stay)
- ORIGINAL letter issued by an Iranian company or employer concerning the job position, the duration of working relationship as well as the purpose of the trip to Austria along, the last 6 months pay slip and the NO. and address of the company in English or its translation in German or English
- Original letter issued by an Iranian University/school and leave of absence for student.
- Letter showing that the applicant sufficient funds wrote by the applicant for unemployment applicant.
- Travel ticket (Reserving the round-trip ticket) Note: The submission of the purchased ticket is not principally required
- Hotel booking/reservation as per the intended trip
- The itinerary as per the intended trip
- Documents showing that the applicant possesses sufficient funds- Recent 3 months bank statements and turn over.
- Under-age visitors who travel alone: Original and copy of the Notary issued Parent/Guardian's Consent Letter or court order
- Original and copy of the Under-age visitor’s birth certificate
- Copy of the father’s/mother’s Schengen visa for Under-age visitors
- Documents showing that the applicant’s Iran residency with at least 3 months validity more than the validity of the requested visa for applicant does not have Iranian citizenship.
- Visa application processing fee must be paid in euros. It should be noted in the case that the application is rejected, the paid fee will not be refunded
- Please click here to know more about the Visa Application Guideline
visa_types.visa_fees:

[Table:  Individual Traveler ]

Please Note: 
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of Euro 31.76 per application in addition to the visa fees and must be paid in cash.
- All fees are non-refundable.
- Applicants are requested to carry exact change in Euros to pay the fees for services used at the Visa Application Centre.
Please note: The fees are to be paid in Euro and the notes must be undamaged.

visa_types.documents_required:
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please
click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/irn/en/aut/travel-insurance)
Download Checklist
- Documents provided by the Visa Applicants must be in German or English
- The application form which must be fully and legibly completed in German (in Latin letters) or in English by the applicant
- 01 color photos (not older than 6 months) (4.5 x 3.5 cm)
click here for photo specification guide (http://www.passbildkriterien.at/oesterreich_neu.html)
- Valid passport (with at least 3months validity more than the validity of the requested visa, with at least 2 free pages, and not more than 10 years since its date of issuance)
- Original of previous passport
- Copy of the applicant's passport (the information page) and Copy of Previous Schengen visas
- Original work/job certificate in English or its translation in German or English
- Copy Travel insurance policy (covering at least 30,000 euros, SARS-COVID 2 and repayment for the entire duration of stay)

- Original letter issued by an Iranian University/school and leave of absence for student.
- Letter showing that the applicant sufficient funds wrote by the applicant for unemployment applicant.
- Travel ticket (Reserving the round-trip ticket) Note: The submission of the purchased ticket is not principally required
- Documents showing that the applicant possesses sufficient funds- Recent 3 months bank statements and turn over.An invitation letter from a company headquartered in Austria and the applicant is going to visit it during his/her business trip. The submitted invitation letter shall specify the travel time, name and passport NO. of the applicant, especially the purpose of travel and accommodation address (if not specified otherwise)
- In the case that the applicant fails to provide sufficient evidence for the purpose of the visit: E-business invitation (EVE or GVE) the inviting company shall set up the invitation letter before the police in the region where the company's headquarter is located.
- This invitation will be sent automatically to the Embassy by the Austrian police. Applicants are kindly ask to provide the number of that EVE resp. GVE when lodging the application.
- Document showing the accommodation address or hotel booking.
- Documents showing the relationship between the applicant and their family, if they travel with him/her.
- Documents showing that the applicant’s Iran residency with at least 3months validity more than the validity of the requested visa for applicant dose not have Iranian citizenship.
- Visa application processing fee must be paid in euros. It should be noted in the case that the application is rejected, the paid fee will not be refunded
- Please click here to know more about the Visa Application Guideline

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
You qualify for this category if you have an invitation from a company in Austria. That invitation can either be an electronic invitation (EVE/GVE) or a traditional letter of invitation.Please be informed that participation of fairs, congresses, workshops, cultural events, etc. do not fall under this category and are considered as individual travel. 
Kindly refer to the documents required page to make sure that you have all the supporting documents.
- Minors under 18 are definitely not accepted at business slots.
- The applicants who want to accompany business travelers have to apply as tourists and not as business travelers.
visa_types.full_text:

The applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

- Finally,       the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.       Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120       Kbytes.
Resolution 
- An       image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used       to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better       printing quality.
- Photos       resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this       parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution,       the bigger the picture.
- The       only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format).       The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be       rejected.
Attaching the Photo to the Application form 
- Glue       one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their  parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father,  one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the  father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
 Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not  meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 10 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
